steps.title: Backsolving choice B
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($p = \frac{10}{3}s + 8$):
Plug in $s=3$:
$$P = \frac{10}{3}\cdot 3 + 8 = 10 + 8 = 18$$
Expected $P=8$, so choice B fails.

steps.title: Backsolving choice C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C ($p = \frac{10}{3}s - 2$):
Plug in $s=3$:
$$P = \frac{10}{3}\cdot 3 - 2 = 10 - 2 = 8$$
Matches given $P=8$.
Check $s=6$:
$$P = \frac{10}{3}\cdot 6 - 2 = 20 - 2 = 18$$
Matches given $P=18$.
Thus choice C fits all points.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
